title: Fimbristylis aspera
taxonomy: Fimbristylis aspera is classified within the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family consists of approximately 5,000 species of plants. The genus Fimbristylis belongs to the order Poales, which includes other monocots such as grasses and rushes. Fimbristylis aspera is further classified into the order Poales, the class Liliopsida, and the division Magnoliophyta. Within the family Cyperaceae, Fimbristylis aspera is part of the tribe Sphaeocephalideae.
uses: Fimbristylis aspera, also known as rough fringe sedge, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating wounds and infections. In some cultures, it is used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its purported expectorant properties. Additionally, the plant is thought to have antiseptic and antioxidant properties, which may aid in protecting against cell damage and promoting overall health. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Fimbristylis aspera for medicinal purposes.

side_effects: Fimbristylis aspera, a plant commonly found in tropical regions, may cause side effects if ingested due to its toxic properties.
